title: sabina vulgaris var. jarkendensis
intro: Sabina vulgaris var. jarkendensis, also known as Himalayan pine or Jarkhand pine, is a variant of the Pinus wallichiana species, native to the Himalayan region. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Sabina vulgaris var. jarkendensis include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of this plant include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
